allow: Bash(python scripts/query_llms.py "You are a senior academic reviewer for a hydraulic engineering master's thesis. The thesis is titled 'Systematic CFD Screening of Guide-Vane Designs for a Rim-Driven Thruster Booster in Pumped-Storage Hydropower' \(Store2Hydro project, NTNU\). Please provide structured, constructive academic feedback on the current state of the thesis based on the following chapter summaries. The author is aware that Theory/Methodology and Results/Discussion are still incomplete. Focus on: \(1\) structural and logical coherence of what is already written, \(2\) gaps or weaknesses in the written text, \(3\) what is missing or should be strengthened before submission. Do NOT suggest adding new research - only comment on improving or completing existing content. --- CHAPTER 1 \(INTRODUCTION\): Strong motivation section on PSH, RPT retrofits, cavitation barriers and the RDT booster concept. Research questions are clearly formulated \(4 RQs around swirl reduction, metrics, bend effects, design ranking\). Scope section defines three key constraints: RPT decoupled, single operating point, steady RANS for screening. The chapter explicitly connects to prior project work \(Leonsen 2025\). One notable gap: a placeholder exists for an RDT motor figure that is not yet inserted. --- CHAPTER 2 \(LITERATURE REVIEW\): Covers booster-RPT systems, multi-objective stator optimisation \(Pareto fronts\), RDT wakes, pre-rotation sensitivity of RPT, swirl decay in straight pipes, bend secondary flow \(Dean vortices\), cascade design \(solidity, diffusion factor, deviation\), CFD methods \(SST k-omega, GCI\), and rotor-stator interaction \(Tyler-Sofrin\). The review is well-referenced and directly connected to the RQs. Each section ends with implications for the present thesis. The structure is logical and cumulative. --- CHAPTER 3 \(THEORY AND METHODOLOGY\): Part I theory is largely written. Covers: governing equations \(continuity and RANS\), cylindrical coordinate description, velocity triangles, hydrofoil geometry, swirl metrics \(swirl number S, angle-based swirl, swirling strength lambda_ci\), cavitation \(NPSH, Thoma number\), cascade theory \(solidity, diffusion factor, de Haller criterion, incidence, deviation\), Dean vortex theory for 90-degree bend, turbulence model \(SST k-omega\) justification, and rotor-stator interaction \(Tyler-Sofrin analysis\). Part II methodology \(design matrix, CFX setup, GCI study, mesh strategy, analysis planes, spectral methodology\) is partially written but still incomplete. Notable written elements: 18-case design matrix \(2 chord strategies CC/CS x 6 chord levels x 3 vane counts\), NoGV reference, multi-domain CFX setup description \(rotating RDT domain + stationary GV domain + stationary draft-tube domain\), and some solver settings. Missing: full GCI study presentation, complete mesh quality metrics, analysis-plane definitions, and spectral methodology details. --- CHAPTER 4 \(RESULTS AND DISCUSSION\): The transient/FFT analysis section is well-written and complete: two transient runs \(CC_450_7 and CC_400_6\), Tyler-Sofrin prediction tested, breathing mode vs spinning mode comparison, physical interpretation using large axial gap and non-uniform inflow. The chapter abstract overview is still a placeholder. The main steady-state screening results are NOT yet written: no Pareto front analysis, no swirl-number comparisons across planes, no bend interaction analysis, no design ranking, and no discussion of which configuration is recommended. --- CHAPTER 5 \(CONCLUSIONS\): Only a placeholder template. Not written. --- CHAPTER 6 \(FUTURE WORK\): Only a placeholder template. Not written. Please give detailed, actionable academic feedback under these headings: A\) What is working well and should be preserved, B\) Structural or logical weaknesses in the written text, C\) Priority improvements needed before the steady-state results are added, D\) How the incomplete sections should connect to the already-written FFT analysis and RQs.")

Bash(python scripts/query_llms.py "You are a senior hydraulic engineering academic reviewer. A colleague \(the original council\) reviewed a master's thesis and gave feedback. A more thorough fact-checker then went through the thesis file and found that several council claims were wrong because the council had not read the full Chapter 3. The thesis is about CFD screening of guide-vane designs for a rim-driven thruster booster in pumped-storage hydropower. Please evaluate whether you agree or disagree with the fact-checker's corrections to the council feedback. Here is what the fact-checker found: \(1\) Council claimed Chapter 3 Part II methodology was incomplete and missing GCI study, analysis planes, mesh quality, and spectral methodology. FACT-CHECKER says: This is WRONG. The file contains complete sections for: design matrix with tables, design space visualisation, computational domain with figure, boundary conditions table, TurboGrid mesh sections, a full GCI triplet study with coarse/medium/fine meshes showing apparent order p and GCI values below 2 percent acceptance threshold, analysis planes table, monitor setup table, spectral methodology including DFT, Hanning window, periodicity check, ring-coherence diagnostic, and peak identification. The council simply had not read these sections. \(2\) Council claimed the rationale for choosing CC_450_7 and CC_400_6 for transient runs was not explained. FACT-CHECKER says: This is WRONG. Section rsi-thesis contains an explicit paragraph titled 'Selection of the m_min >= 1 counterpart' explaining exactly why CC_400_6 was chosen instead of CC_450_6, specifically that the Tyler-Sofrin mode order depends on Zs not chord, that the 50 mm chord difference changes solidity by only 11 percent versus 17 percent from the vane count change, and that CC_400_6 is more representative of the centre of the screening matrix. \(3\) Council claimed GCI study was missing and called it 'non-negotiable'. FACT-CHECKER says: This is COMPLETELY WRONG. The GCI study is more thorough than most master theses: full triplet \(coarse/medium/fine\), apparent order, GCI12 and GCI23 values, acceptance threshold below 2 percent, two verification configurations \(straight-flow and swirling-flow\), and a section picking the medium mesh. \(4\) On operating point justification \(ṁ=290.3 kg/s, n=650 rpm\): Council said begrunnelse was missing. FACT-CHECKER partially agrees: the mass flow is justified in the text as the Store2Hydro programme design point. But one sentence explicitly linking n=650 rpm to the blade-passing frequency used in the transient analysis is missing. \(5\) On analysis plane diagram: Council partially correct. A domain layout figure exists showing geometry and dimensions, and a GCI-planes figure exists showing planes in GCI context only. But a combined figure showing ALL analysis planes on the full domain \(used as main reference for all 18 configurations, not just GCI\) would genuinely help the reader. \(6\) On mesh quality table: Council partially correct. y+ values, face angle, expansion ratio, inflation layers are documented in prose and bullet lists, but not gathered in one summary table. A table would improve scannability for the examiner. \(7\) On CC vs CS design rationale: Council correct. The rationale is spread across multiple sections. A short paragraph consolidating the choice before the design matrix would help. Please tell me: A\) Do you agree with the fact-checker that claims 1, 2, and 3 were major errors on the council's part? B\) For claims 4-7, which does a senior examiner actually care about most? C\) Any other observations about this situation - specifically, what does it say about how to use AI councils for academic review?")
